name: How is fault determined in work chemical exposure cases?
description: No-fault in workers' comp—benefits if job-related.

mainEntity.name: How is fault determined in work chemical exposure cases?

acceptedAnswer.text: No-fault in workers' comp—benefits if job-related.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Workers' comp is no-fault; you get benefits regardless of employer negligence unless intentional.** Fault matters only for third-party suits (e.g., chemical supplier).[1][6] Key factors: pure work-relatedness proven by evidence. No contributory negligence bar in comp; focus on causation. Attorney identifies liable third parties for extra damages.
